steps.title: Ignore the interrupting clause
steps.content: The subject is the singular food writer J. Kenji Lopez-Alt. The clause "who explores the science behind cooking" interrupts the subject and verb but does not change the needed agreement.

steps.title: Reject plural verb forms
steps.content: Choice B uses the plural auxiliary "are." Choice C uses the plural base verb. Choice D uses the plural auxiliary "have." A singular subject needs a singular present-tense verb. The answer is A.
